top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 87, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Göteborgsgirot's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Göteborgsgirot. Train it until it feels boring.
At Göteborgsgirot, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Göteborgsgirot's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 87 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Göteborgsgirot's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 87 miles.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 87 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 87 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Göteborgsgirot's mixed terrain actually does to you.
Tempo is Göteborgsgirot's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 87 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.heat: May in Gothenburg, Sweden can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Göteborgsgirot. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Göteborgsgirot’s 87 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and G Spot to prepare you for 87 miles of mixed terrain in Gothenburg, Sweden.
